sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: LEFT, SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 27, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: SPACE only changed 1 cell - created a white trail, didn't activate the box. The player's columns (33-35) don't perfectly align with the box's columns (32-35). First successful SPACE had perfect column alignment.
- Plan: Go LEFT to align columns with the box, then SPACE.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 26 of about 71 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
SPACE had no impact (no_impact: True). Player moved LEFT to [40-43, 29-31], which is now to the LEFT of the box at [36-39, 32-35]. The SPACE didn't do anything because the player is no longer below the box.
I need to rethink my approach. Let me reconsider what SPACE does:
1. First SPACE: player at [29-31, 44-47], box above at [24-27, 44-47] → box turned white (12 cells changed)
2. Second SPACE: player at [37-39, 36-39], no box above → new box appeared at [32-35, 36-39] (12 cells changed)
3. Third SPACE: player at [40-43, 33-35], box above at [36-39, 32-35] → only 1 cell changed (white trail to left)
4. Fourth SPACE: player at [40-43, 29-31], no box above → no impact
So SPACE behavior seems to be:
- If there's a box directly above with PERFECT alignment (same columns), activate it (turn white)
- If there's no box above, create a new box above
- If there's a box above but NOT perfectly aligned, create a white trail (partial effect)
The player moved too far left. I need to get back to being directly below the box at [36-39, 32-35]. The player is at [40-43, 29-31], need to go RIGHT to align with cols 32-35.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole puzzle. Maybe the goal isn't about activating boxes at all. Let me look at the initial state more carefully.
Initial state had:
- Left box: [28-31, 16-19] (charcoal with blue center)
- Horizontal bar: [28-31, 28-39] (gray with blue center)
- Top-right box: [24-27, 44-47] (dark gray with blue center)
- Player: [29-31, 44-47] (green)
After first SPACE, the top-right box turned white. Then I created a new box by mistake.
Wait, maybe I should reconsider the goal entirely. What if the goal is to make all boxes the same color? Or what if the goal is to create a specific pattern?
Let me go back to basics. I need to get below the bottom-center box and align properly. The box is at [36-39, 32-35]. I need to be at rows 40-43, cols 32-35. Currently at [40-43, 29-31]. Need to go RIGHT 3 times to get to cols 32-35.
Actually, the player is 3 cols wide (29-31), and the box is 4 cols wide (32-35). They can't perfectly align if they have different widths. Let me think about this differently.
Maybe the player's width changes depending on position? Or maybe I need to be at a specific offset.
Let me just try going RIGHT to get back under the box and try SPACE again.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: SPACE had no impact - player at [40-43, 29-31] is too far left from box at [36-39, 32-35]. Need to realign. SPACE seems to require perfect column alignment with box above.
Plan: Go RIGHT to get back under the bottom-center box, then try SPACE again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at 40-43, 29-31
# Box at 36-39, 32-35
# Need to go RIGHT to align
# Player is 3 cols wide, box is 4 cols wide
# Try RIGHT to get under the box

result = action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
print(f"RIGHT result: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Green at rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], px={n['pixels']}")

# Check box
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 12:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"Box at rows [{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
